prompt: JOptionPane.showInputDialog always returns null. What is the bug?
choices: showInputDialog requires a parent component as the first argument
In headless mode there is no display; showInputDialog cannot show a dialog and returns null; calling .length() on null throws NullPointerException
JOptionPane.showInputDialog returns an empty string, not null, when cancelled
showInputDialog must be called on the EDT to return a non-null value
explanation: In a headless environment (java.awt.headless=true), there is no screen or input device. JOptionPane cannot display a dialog and returns null. Similarly, if a user cancels the dialog, null is returned. Calling .length() on null throws NullPointerException. Fix: always null-check the return value before using it.
